name: Trey McBride
headline: The undisputed TE1, even with touchdown regression looming
body:
McBride isn't a tight end so much as a featured No. 1 receiver who happens to line up inline. He posted a 27.4% target share on 169 looks in 2025, ran a route on 94.1% of dropbacks, and turned that volume into 126 catches for 1,239 yards. The underlying efficiency backs the workload: a 0.436 EPA per play, a 60.4% success rate, and just two drops on 128 catchable balls. He's been a target magnet for three straight years, and at 25 the usage floor is rock solid.

The only real caution is the 11 touchdowns, which more than quintupled his 2024 total of two and won't fully repeat. Even halved, his receiving profile keeps him in the elite tier, and his 33 red-zone targets argue he'll stay involved near the goal line. A new coaching staff and added backfield investment could trim Arizona's league-leading pass volume, but no tight end pairs this target share with this efficiency. Draft him as the position's clear top option.
